title: Chelan Butte XC
description:
A big June cross-country from Chelan Butte out over the Waterville Plateau and back. Strong thermals off the Columbia breaks took the wing to cloudbase again and again, with cores well over +4 m/s on the plateau edge.

Late afternoon the cumulus started spreading out and overdeveloping to the east, so it turned into a race to stay in the sun on the final glides. Landed on the flats just as a rain cell drifted through — a classic Chelan XC day.

media.caption: Wing laid out on Chelan Butte, ready to launch
Lake Chelan stretching back into the Cascades
Cloudbase building over the Cascade foothills
The Columbia River gorge on course
Out over the Waterville Plateau wheat fields
Late glide under spreading clouds and distant virga
Landed on the flats as a rain cell drifts past
